feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: NBA Draft picks tracker 2026: Live results, updates, list of selections from Rounds 1-2
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live results for every pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, from the start of Round 1 through No. 60.

entry.title: Pros and cons of New York Knicks picking Vanderbilt's Tyler Nickel in 2026 NBA Draft
entry.content: Tyler Nickel of Vanderbilt basketball was selected in the 2026 NBA Draft 47th overall by the New York Knicks.

entry.title: Dallas Mavericks draft picks tracker 2026: Live results, grades, updates, list of selections and trades
entry.content: The Mavs had the top pick a year ago and took Cooper Flagg.

entry.title: Acquiring Braden Smith for Kam Jones in NBA Draft strengthens Pacers' backcourt
entry.content: The Pacers traded the No. 38 pick in 2025 in Kam Jones for the No. 38 pick in 2026 for Braden Smith, but that looks like an upgrade for Indiana.

entry.title: Acquiring Braden Smith for Kam Jones in NBA Draft strengthens Pacers' backcourt
entry.content: The Pacers traded the No. 38 pick in 2025 in Kam Jones for the No. 38 pick in 2026 for Braden Smith, but that looks like an upgrade for Indiana.

entry.title: Duke's Isaiah Evans drafted by Brooklyn Nets with 33rd overall pick
entry.content: After two seasons with Duke, Isaiah Evans is on to the NBA, being selected No. 33 overall by the Brooklyn Nets.

entry.title: Capitals land Sabres' Alex Tuch in sign-and-trade deal: Top NHL free agents left
entry.content: Top NHL free agent Alex Tuch is off the board, heading to the Capitals in a deal with the Sabres. Here are the best remaining free agents.

entry.content: Coming off a championship, this is a chance for the Knicks to get better.

entry.title: Jaguars roster: Where does DeeJay Dallas fit in backfield?
entry.content: DeeJay Dallas enters training camp looking to carve out a role in the Jaguars' crowded backfield, with special teams likely playing a key part.

entry.content: Veesaar appears to have gotten this one wrong.

entry.content: Mexico, already winner of Group A, take on Czechia in the final group stage game. Here is their starting lineup.

entry.title: World Cup: Brazil crowned Group C winners as Scotland face nervous wait
entry.content: With a 3-0 victory over Scotland this evening in a humid Miami Stadium, Brazil cemented their place in the knockout stages of the 2026 World Cup as winners of Group C. Meanwhile, Scotland face a nervo...

entry.title: The Lurid Lawsuit, Salami Scandal and Trash-Can Thief Vexing JPMorgan’s PR Dept.
entry.content: The country’s biggest bank landed in the news again after an employee was caught taking a Knicks-themed trash can.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Hardly anyone knows how much is gained by ignoring the future.
Lucky Numbers: 67, 42, 41, 41, 63, 21

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: If you purchase something that comes with a unique charger/power cable, label it with what it's for.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: In France, a five year old child can buy an alcoholic drink in a bar

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Lao Tzu
entry.content: “The best fighter is never angry.” — Lao Tzu

entry.title: 7.1 magnitude earthquake strikes Venezuela, triggers tsunami advisory
entry.content: A preliminary 7.1 magnitude earthquake was reported Wednesday evening in northwest Venezuela, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.

entry.title: Rams backup QB battle has some worried about Ty Simpson's development
entry.content: Is Ty Simpson not developing under the Rams system?

entry.title: How to live stream Yankees vs Tigers: MLB, TV channel
entry.content: The New York Yankees and Detroit Tigers wrap up their three-game series Wednesday night at Comerica Park in a matchup featuring two left-handed starters heading in opposite directions. MORE: Popular ‘fried chicken’ Yankees dessert is major hit with MLB fans…

entry.title: USMNT star Christian Pulisic’s future in doubt after ‘very attractive’ MLS approach during World Cup
entry.content: United States men’s national team star Christian Pulisic may have an important decision to make during the 2026 World Cup. Pulisic started in the USA’s 4-1 group-stage victory over Paraguay almost a fortnight ago, assisting one of Folarin Balogun’s two goals.

entry.title: Alonso’s first Chelsea signing set to be announced after hijack transfer deal
entry.content: Marco Palestra to Chelsea: Xabi Alonso’s First Transfer SignalChelsea appear ready to make Marco Palestra the first signing of the Xabi Alonso era, and there is a logic to it that feels very modern,...

entry.title: 4-star DL Kadin Fife decommits from Tennessee
entry.content: Centre (Ala.) Cherokee County four-star defensive lineman Kadin Fife has decommitted from Tennessee. He was pledged to the Volunteers since Nov. 21, but is now reopening his recruitment ahead of his senior season. Fife is the No. 363 overall prospect and No. 37 DL in the 2027 cycle, according to the Rivals Industry Ranking, a weighted […]

entry.title: USA soccer round of 32 opponent is almost surely Bosnia and Herzegovina
entry.content: The United States men's national team will almost certainly play Bosnia and Herzegovina in the FIFA World Cup round of 32.

entry.title: Swiss beat qualified Canada to snatch top spot, Bosnians left to hope
entry.content: Co-hosts Canada qualified for the men's World Cup knock-out stages for the first time but lost out on top spot and a Vancouver last-32 game to Switzerland after a 2-1 defeat on Wednesday. Second-half goals from Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi meant the Swiss finished top of Group B on seven points and the Canadians ended up in second spot on four, above Bosnia and Herzegovina on goal difference, despite Promise David's strike to cut the deficit.

entry.title: European Space Agency's Euclid captures the star-filled center of the Milky Way
entry.content: NASA will begin mapping the galactic bulge with a mission later this summer.

entry.title: England beat West Indies to secure semi-final place
entry.content: England ease into the T20 World Cup semi-finals with a game to spare by comprehensively beating West Indies by 38 runs at Lord's.

entry.title: The Owens family curse strikes the next generation in the new 'Practical Magic 2' trailer
entry.content: The new 'Practical Magic 2' trailer sees Sally's (Sandra Bullock) daughter Kylie (Joey King) learning about magic and the Owens family curse.

entry.title: Shipbuilding hurdles impede Navy's ability to keep pace with well-equipped adversaries, experts warn
entry.content: President Trump's proposed "Golden Fleet," a major modernization and expansion initiative that he announced in December 2025, aims to aggressively scale up American naval dominance and reinvigorate the country's domestic shipbuilding industry. Analysts say the maritime transformation will be crucial in the event of hostilities with China.

entry.title: Jalapeño is the first AI chip from OpenAI and Broadcom
entry.content: It's a spicy start for the AI company's collaboration with the chip maker.

entry.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
entry.content: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.

entry.title: Red Sox's 'unlikely' trade candidate named potential target for Mets
entry.content: This would be a strange deal for the Red Sox to make.

entry.title: Why Red Sox are unlikely to trade $87.5 million star slugger
entry.content: This veteran slugger is not likely to be moved for a simple reason.

entry.title: Latest mock links Royals to the fastest player in the entire draft class
entry.content: "Speed kills" certainly still applies to the game today.

entry.title: Novig Promo Code TSNEWS50: Claim $50 Bonus for World Cup Prediction Markets
entry.content: Play $5 after signing up with the Novig promo code TSNEWS50 and gain $50 in Novig Coins for World Cup predictions.

entry.title: A Turkish Arms Sale Leads to a Face-Off Between Trump and Congress
entry.content: The administration is expected to push for a rare override to get the deal through as the NATO summit in Ankara approaches.

entry.title: Phillies might have a problem in a potential trade for Red Sox closer Aroldis Chapman
entry.content: Chapman would be great, but there's just one problem for the Phillies.

entry.title: Culture Clash knows the end is near. It wants to go out with a bang
entry.content: "We are highly pissed off about a lot of stuff right now," said comic Richard Montoya. Come June 27, his comedy crew Culture Clash will take the Grand Performances stage with sketches colored by political and social satire.

entry.title: Dodgers may 'salivate' over 8X All-Star closer from Red Sox as Edwin Diaz replacement
entry.content: The Dodgers and Red Sox are logical trade partners.

entry.title: Brighton working on deal to sign Lia Walti from Juventus
entry.content: Brighton & Hove Albion are working on a deal to sign midfielder Lia Walti from Juventus in a move which would mark her return to the Women’s Super League. The 33-year-old has undertaken a medical and although the deal is not yet finalised, negotiations between the clubs continue to progress positively. Walti was keen to return to England and Juventus were open to finding a solution despite only signing her from Arsenal on a two-year contract last September. Under head coach Massimiliano Canzi, W

entry.title: If available, the Rockets shouldn’t hesitate to draft this former UH star
entry.content: There’s plenty of talent left in this draft, but one name sticks out as a possible option.

entry.title: FACT FOCUS: Norway brought its own food to the World Cup. But not because it distrusts US products
entry.content: As Norway excels in its first World Cup appearance since 1998, false claims about what the team is eating are also grabbing attention online. The allegations focus on the quality of American food — more specifically, that the Norwegians distrust it so much that they brought food from home to avoid eating it. It's true that the team shipped certain products from Norway for the World Cup, but the reason has nothing to do with concerns about quality.

entry.title: UC Bearcats to play top 2027 event; returning slugger honored again
entry.content: Cincinnati Bearcats baseball will play in an event with Tennessee, Georgia Tech and Illinois in 2027. Plus, slugger Quinton Coats adds another honor.

entry.title: Bosnia 2-1 up against Qatar and Switzerland level with co-hosts Canada in World Cup
entry.content: Follow live as Switzerland face Canada and Bosnia-Herzegovina play Qatar in the final round of World Cup Group B fixtures.

entry.title: Alex Tuch trade grades: Who won Capitals and Sabres sign-and-trade deal?
entry.content: Alex Tuch is off to the Washington Capitals from the Buffalo Sabres. Let's hand out some grades.

entry.title: MBA, CPA named among top ranked high schools in Tennessee
entry.content: Seven Nashville area high schools rank among the top 25 schools for athletes in the state of Tennessee, according to a study.

entry.title: Seahawks RB Jadarian Price could be fantasy football dynasty option
entry.content: Seahawks rookie Jadarian Price has the makings to be a high-end option at running back for fantasy football team managers in dynasty leagues.

entry.title: USMNT's Christian Pulisic offers injury update ahead of Türkiye game after missing Australia win
entry.content: The U.S. Men's National Team heads into their final group stage game against Türkiye with Christian Pulisic potentially set to play a role once more, having recovered sufficiently from a calf injury

entry.title: A hot mess, 'Supergirl' has a rowdy spirit but a too-terrestrial plot
entry.content: Craig Gillespie directs the louche party girl, played by Milly Alcock, in a spinoff sequel that thrives on chaos but settles for convention.

entry.title: Here’s why Slate changed the battery in its cheap EV truck
entry.content: While there was probably a moment when Slate’s leadership had to green-light the switch from one battery type to another, the momentum toward that decision had been building for years.

entry.title: Fever's Caitlin Clark gets big news about All-Star starter hopes
entry.content: Fever and WNBA fans have spoken.

entry.title: Canada vs. Switzerland live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup Group B match
entry.content: Having confirmed a place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout round, co-hosts Canada now seek the top spot in Group B as Les Rouges take on Switzerland in Vancouver on Wednesday to conclude group stage play.

entry.title: Lakers, Spurs named top landing spots for declining $100 million Warriors four-time NBA champion
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers were linked to a Golden State Warriors forward who isn't the same player he used to be.

entry.title: Top 30 2026 NHL UFAs: Alex Tuch, Rasmus Andersson, John Carlsson highlight free agent board
entry.content: NHL free agency begins on July 1, just one week away. The UFA class doesn't feature the most exciting names of all time, but their is still plenty of talent across the board.

entry.title: New draft projections have Athletics solving major roster problem at No. 8
entry.content: This Arkansas slugger could be an excellent fit.

entry.title: Phillies predicted to trade for $151M Alec Bohm replacement from Giants
entry.content: This would be a massive deal for the Phillies financially and competitively.

entry.title: Braves trade prediction lands Tarik Skubal at huge two-thirds top prospect cost
entry.content: This trade prediction would be exciting, but a little worrying for the Braves.

entry.title: Yankees linked to trade deadline reunion with Mets' $22 million reliever
entry.content: The Yankees have an opportunity to reacquire a former fan favorite.

entry.title: China's foreign minister urges swift reopening of Strait of Hormuz for global trade, Xinhua reports
entry.content: China's foreign minister urges swift reopening of Strait of Hormuz for global trade, Xinhua reports

entry.title: Ockenden report: victims' families say babies treated with ‘absence of dignity’ – video
entry.content: Jack Hawkins, whose baby Harriet died at the Nottingham NHS trust, spoke to the media on Wednesday on behalf of bereaved families after the release of Donna Ockenden's findings into the NHS’s biggest maternity scandal. Details revealed that more than 500 mothers and babies suffered potentially avoidable harm or died due to 'deeply embedded systemic failures' at a 'toxic' hospital trustMore than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed at ‘toxic’ Nottingham NHS trust, report findsNottingham maternity care scandal review: what are the key findings?‘Truly horrific’: the stories of five people affected by the NHS maternity scandal Continue reading...

entry.title: Facebook rolls out an AI companion app for creators
entry.content: The new app, which is currently being tested with select creators, will have Facebook's recently launched AI creator assistant built into it.

entry.title: Deezer says its new feature lets fans remix songs with artist consent
entry.content: Global music streaming service Deezer is taking a contrarian approach to AI, even as it adds a feature that lets fans remix songs.

entry.title: ‘Cruel’ care and ‘toxic’ culture: shocking findings in maternity report - The Latest
entry.content: A shocking report into the biggest ever maternity care scandal in the NHS has revealed more than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed as a result of inadequate care in Nottingham. The review, led by senior midwife Donna Ockenden, found there was a dismissive attitude to women’s concerns, failures in maternity care, staff shortages, a toxic culture and racism at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS trust. Lucy Hough speaks to UK health and inequalities correspondent Tobi Thomas. Continue reading...

entry.title: We must be prepared for deadly heatwaves to get worse | Letter
entry.content: Readers react to the brutal heat conditions affecting Britain and Europe Last summer, I wrote to you warning of the growing threat that extreme heat poses to both patients and the NHS, that the demand for healthcare would rise as temperatures climbed, that our hospitals were ill-equipped to cope and that investment in resilience was urgently needed (Letters, 21 July 2025).A year on, the UK is set for another record-breaking heatwave, yet little has changed. The UK Health Security Agency has also taken the rare step of issuing a red heat alert in parts of England – signalling a serious threat to lives (Report, 22 June). This marks only the second time that a red alert has been issued. The last, in 2022, coincided with five waves of extreme heat that combined to cause an estimated 2,985 excess deaths in England alone. Continue reading...

entry.title: Agility Robotics plans to go public via SPAC in a $2.5B deal
entry.content: Agility Robotics, the humanoid robotics startup that spun out of Oregon State University in 2015, expects to generate $620 million in proceeds.

entry.title: Scotland vs Brazil prediction for World Cup group stage match
entry.content: Get a detailed Scotland vs Brazil prediction for today's World Cup group stage match in Miami.

entry.title: Adviser to Former New York City Mayor Eric Adams Charged With Bribery
entry.content: Federal agents arrested Adams’s ex-chief of staff Frank Carone for alleged migrant shelter scheme.

entry.title: NTSB launches probe into fatal Texas Tesla crash
entry.content: The safety board, known for its thorough investigations, is probing the crash alongside the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

entry.title: 'When Calls the Heart's' Erin Krakow and Ben Rosenbaum call baby girl their 'greatest gift'
entry.content: 'When Calls the Heart' co-stars Erin Krakow and Ben Rosenbaum celebrated one year of marriage by publicly introducing the latest member of their growing family.

entry.title: Hundreds of mothers and babies died or harmed at ‘toxic’ hospital trust – The Latest
entry.content: A shocking report into the biggest ever maternity care scandal in the NHS has revealed more than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed as a result of inadequate care in Nottingham. The review, led by the senior midwife Donna Ockenden, found there had been a dismissive attitude to women’s concerns, failures in maternity care, staff shortages, a toxic culture and racism at Nottingham University hospitals NHS trust. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s UK health and inequalities correspondent Tobi Thomas – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

entry.title: Trump cancels plan to sign housing bill until Congress passes election reforms
entry.content: President Trump abruptly canceled the signing of a bipartisan housing bill until Congress is able to pass sweeping election reforms. NBC News' Jonathan Allen reports on the president's decision and the roadblocks the SAVE America Act faces in the Senate.

entry.title: Lakers shower Austin Reaves with biggest payday for undrafted player
entry.content: Austin Reaves sets new benchmark for undrafted players.

entry.title: Wizards' AJ Dybantsa reveals what his first purchase will be with $69 million NBA contract
entry.content: AJ Dybantsa was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

entry.title: NBA Draft 2026 best players available for Round 2: Big board, full draft order heading into Wednesday night
entry.content: With the first round of the draft completed, here's a guide to Wednesday's second round action.

entry.title: Yankees make exciting roster move on 27-year-old fan favorite infielder
entry.content: This is a very fun roster move for the Yankees this summer.

entry.title: Angel Reese and the Atlanta Dream top new WNBA power rankings for a simple reason
entry.content: The Dream has been dominant.

entry.title: Who will USA play next? Updated scenarios for USMNT's Round of 32 opponent from third-place pool
entry.content: The United States have already confirmed their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout bracket, but their Round of 32 opponent has yet to be determined.

entry.title: Royals trade prediction sends $46 million All-Star to White Sox for top 10 prospect
entry.content: Kansas City would be acquiring at least one of Chicago's top 10 prospects.

entry.title: More than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed at ‘toxic’ Nottingham NHS trust, report finds
entry.content: Donna Ockenden inquiry finds ‘bullying’ culture and ‘cruel’, dismissive attitude to women contributed to avoidable deathsOckenden report – latest updatesKey findings from maternity scandal report‘Truly horrific’: five stories from the scandalMore than 500 mothers and babies came to harm or died as a result of inadequate care in Nottingham, an inquiry into the NHS’s biggest ever maternity scandal has revealed.A total of 444 women and 76 newborn babies suffered “potentially avoidable” outcomes because they received substandard treatment over 13 years from Nottingham University hospitals NHS trust (NUH), a damning report led by the childbirth expert Donna Ockenden has found. Continue reading...

entry.title: Ockenden report, as it happened: families call for public inquiry and say babies treated with ‘absence of dignity’
entry.content: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here:Hundreds died or harmed due to ‘toxic’ maternity careTruly horrific’: five stories from the scandalWhat are the key report findings?Presenting some of her findings, Ockenden said her team found “significant or major concerns in care where different or better care may have made a difference to the outcome” in the following cases:21% of cases where mothers died,26% of cases where mothers experienced a major obstetric haemorrhage,36% of cases where a mother had an unplanned admission to intensive care,20% of cases involving a mother’s care when a baby was stillborn,50% of a mother’s care when a baby suffered hypoxic brain injury. Continue reading...

entry.title: Google Home Speaker review: A modest update for the Gemini era
entry.content: Google's new speaker is a modest update, but the question is whether Gemini is a good smart home assistant.

entry.title: Farm workers at higher risk amid screwworm outbreak in US south-west
entry.content: Those who work with animals are at greater risk for infection, but face challenges in accessing healthcareAgricultural workers are among the highest risk group for human infection during the screwworm outbreak in the American south, yet they frequently face challenges in accessing public health – an ongoing concern amid zoonotic spillovers such as H5N1 bird flu.Screwworm has been detected in goats and sheep in three Texas counties in recent days, bringing the total to 16 known cases among animals and none reported in people. Continue reading...

entry.title: US government reportedly urging Meta to share its AI models
entry.content: The US government is reportedly asking Meta to share its AI models for review, in the midst of growing security and safety concerns over the technology.

entry.title: Rockstar finally reveals pricing for Grand Theft Auto 6
entry.content: Grand Theft Auto 6 will cost $80 when pre-orders open on June 25th at midnight.

entry.title: ‘Significant harm’: children’s watchdog decries Home Office plan to push out refused asylum seekers
entry.content: Rachel de Souza writes to home secretary over proposals that include removing sick childrenUK politics – live updatesShabana Mahmood has been told that her crackdown on refused asylum seekers, including the forcible removal of children from the UK, will cause “significant harm”, in an intervention by an independent watchdog.Rachel de Souza, the children’s commissioner for England, said the home secretary’s plan under consultation to push families to leave the UK – including those with children receiving medical treatment – should not be implemented as proposed. Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘Smaller doses of exercise are a miracle cure’: 14 expert tips to protect your joints
entry.content: Life is much easier if you look after your hips, knees, elbows and shoulders – especially as you get older. Rheumatologists and orthopaedic surgeons explain how to work out, what to eat and how to talk to your doctorOur bodies are incredible machines, but we can take the mechanics for granted until something goes wrong. How can we maintain healthy joints throughout life and avoid surgery? Here, rheumatologists and orthopaedic surgeons give their tips … Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘Truly horrific’: the stories of five people affected by the NHS maternity scandal
entry.content: Some see publication of the Nottingham report as a landmark moment, others as bittersweet and traumaticFull story: More than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed at ‘toxic’ NHS trustMaternity care scandal review: what are the key findings?The long-awaited report into maternity failures at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS trust (NUH), the largest investigation of its kind involving about 2,500 families, has been published.Led by the senior midwife Donna Ockenden, the inquiry investigated stillbirths, neonatal deaths, maternal deaths and babies or mothers who suffered brain damage and other injuries between 2012 and 2025. Continue reading...

entry.title: Nottingham maternity care scandal review: what are the key findings?
entry.content: Review of 2,500 cases between 2012 and 2025 finds ‘systemic’ and ‘deep-rooted’ failures, a bullying culture and racismMore than 500 mothers and babies died or were harmed at ‘toxic’ Nottingham NHS trust, report finds‘Truly horrific’: the stories of five people affectedA review into the NHS’s biggest ever maternity care scandal has been published. Led by Donna Ockenden, an independent senior midwife, the review examined 2,500 cases involving mothers and babies dying or being seriously injured, or babies being stillborn, while under the care of Nottingham university hospitals NHS trust between 2012 and 2025. Below is a summary of the findings from the report.• The subheading and text of this article was amended on 24 June 2026. An earlier version said the report reviewed cases from 2012 to 2015; in fact, it covered cases up to 2025. Continue reading...

entry.title: Agility, Maker of Humanlike Robots, to Go Public in $2.5 Billion SPAC Deal
entry.content: Agility’s humanoid robot, Digit, is used at manufacturing facilities and warehouses by companies such as Amazon.

entry.title: ‘It’s dangerous’: how UK schools, care homes and other workplaces are coping in soaring heat
entry.content: From bus drivers struggling to focus, to those hauling scaffolding in direct sun, workers say risks must be taken seriouslyEurope heatwave live – latest updatesWith temperatures in the UK hitting record levels for June, people are being advised to avoid exercise and unnecessary travel. So how do you work in this heat?We look at how various sectors of the economy are coping with unprecedented temperatures, and how working practices will have to adapt to increasingly frequent heatwaves that are predicted to be longer and more intense owing to the global climate emergency.Not all care facilities are created equal Continue reading...
